patch.value:
## Context

With 1032 closed items and growing, periodic history maintenance requires either scripted loops or a built-in pass. The existing `pm history-compact --before <all>` (no `--before` arg = compact everything) works per-item but there is no corpus-wide sweep.

Related to pm-f3pa (bulk compaction by count threshold) but distinct: this is about targeting CLOSED items as a corpus-level maintenance pass.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `pm history-compact --scope closed` (or `--all-closed`) compacts all closed items to a single baseline entry
- [ ] Skips streams that are already at 1-3 entries (already compact)
- [ ] Reports: items_compacted, items_skipped, items_errored
- [ ] `--dry-run` mode shows plan without writing
- [ ] Integrated with `pm health --fix` hint or `pm gc --scope history` alias

## Evidence

- `src/cli/commands/history-compact.ts:214-399` — complete single-item compaction, no bulk mode
- `node dist/cli.js stats --storage` — 17,753 entries across 1,063 streams; corpus is 19.5MB and growing
pm history-compact: expose --scope all-streams (history GC pass) for closed items

For closed items that will never be mutated again, it is safe to compact the entire history stream to a single baseline snapshot. There is no first-class 'compact all closed' operation, leaving growing history debt as the project ages. The all-streams bulk path could skip already-minimal streams (1-2 entries) and only act on streams over a threshold, making a full pass fast.

- `pm history-compact --scope closed` (or `--all-closed`) compacts all closed items to a single baseline entry
- Skips streams that are already at 1-3 entries (already compact)
- Reports: items_compacted, items_skipped, items_errored
- `--dry-run` mode shows plan without writing
- Integrated with `pm health --fix` hint or `pm gc --scope history` alias

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Shipped as part of the unified bulk path: --scope closed compacts only terminal items (lifecycle classifier), --scope all-streams sweeps every stream. Skips already-compact streams via --min-entries (default 3). Orphan streams (history file, no item) are unbucketed and excluded from scope=closed. Reports items_compacted/items_skipped/items_errored.

patch.value: Delivered via the unified bulk path: --scope closed/all-streams corpus sweep with min-entries skip and orphan-stream handling.
Delivered via the unified bulk path: --scope closed/all-streams corpus sweep with min-entries skip and orphan-stream handling.
